title_original: Quantum Machines Establishes Flagship Hub at Illinois IQMP
summary_original: Quantum Machines will establish its flagship research and development hub at the Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park (IQMP) in Chicago, according to a joint announcement with the Illinois Economic Development Corporation. Supported by grant funding from the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, Quantum Machines will operate from the IQMP’s On-Ramp facilities, offering over 2,000 square feet of specialized quantum labs. The lab will advance hardware-control co-design for scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computing, deploying their OPX1000 controller to support superconducting and spin qubits. Partnerships include local workforce initiatives, collaboration with Diraq for silicon-based quantum computers, and hosting the 2026 Adaptive Quantum Circuits conference in Chicago. The 128-acre IQMP aims to accelerate the commercialization of quantum technologies.
